summary: Diff adds new e-commerce pages (Shop, Orders, Vendor) and an OrderDetailDialog wired to React Query hooks, adds routes/nav links for these pages, and gates getAllAnalytics usage behind admin checks on Marketplace and Discovery pages. However, the diff excerpt for useQueries.ts is truncated, so the presence of *all* required new e-commerce hooks and correct cache invalidation behavior cannot be fully verified from the provided evidence. Several unrelated “enhanced/AI” and admin/wallet expansions appear in modified component summaries beyond the stated BuildRequest.

missing_requirements.requirement: useQueries.ts exports query/mutation hooks for each listed new e-commerce backend method (createVendor, getMyVendor, getVendorById, createProduct, updateProduct, listProducts, listProductsByVendor, createOrder, getOrder, updateOrderStatus, listMyOrders, listOrdersByVendor, creditVendor, getMyVendorBalance, withdrawVendorBalance) using the existing useActor() pattern, with hooks disabled when actor is unavailable, and mutations invalidating the correct cached queries.
missing_requirements.reason: The provided diff content for frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts is truncated before any e-commerce hook implementations are shown, so it’s not possible to confirm that all listed hooks exist, follow the useActor() pattern, are properly disabled when the actor is unavailable, and perform the required query invalidations.
evidence: frontend-file-summaries.txt (claims completeness but does not show hook names/invalidation details)

missing_requirements.requirement: AdminPanel continues to use getAllAnalytics and displays analytics normally for admins.
missing_requirements.reason: MarketplacePage and DiscoveryHubPage are shown to gate useGetAllAnalytics behind admin checks, but no diff excerpt shows AdminPanel’s analytics fetching behavior, so continuing admin-only analytics behavior cannot be confirmed from the provided evidence.
evidence: frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx (not included in diff excerpt; only described in frontend-file-summaries.txt)

hallucinated_features.description: “Enhanced AI-powered discovery engine” / expanded recommendation scoring logic beyond the requested admin-only analytics gating and safe defaults.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-3 only asks to prevent non-admin calls to admin-only analytics and to render gracefully with safe defaults. The diff introduces extensive new AI-like scoring factors and recommendation logic not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Large-scale AdminPanel expansion (overview, revenue automation, wallet management with instant settlement tracking, AI-personalized rewards engine configuration, intelligent advertising platform setup, system health monitoring, economic transparency).
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest explicitly limits scope to wiring the React frontend to new backend endpoints and fixing admin-only analytics calls; it also states not to redesign overall UX beyond what’s required. These AdminPanel feature expansions are not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Multi-currency wallet enhancements (real-time ICP + fiat balances, instant settlement support, expanded transaction processing).
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by REQ-1/2/3; wallet is outside the e-commerce wiring and analytics gating scope described.
